topic: Someone finds out their best friend is moving across the country
raw_output:
[story 1]

The glossy flyer, tucked haphazardly into the corner of my backpack, felt like a physical assault. "Moving to Seattle!" The bold lettering screamed across the page, and my stomach did a slow, unpleasant flip. I stared at the text, my mouth suddenly dry. Liam. My Liam. He was actually relocating to the Pacific Northwest? The reality of it settled over me like a heavy, damp blanket. I quickly shoved the paper deep into my bag, my fingers fumbling with the zipper, desperate to hide the evidence of this monumental shift in our friendship. When Liam finally walked up, grinning that familiar, easy grin, I managed a weak, forced laugh that sounded far too high-pitched. "Hey, you ready for the road trip?" I asked, my voice cracking slightly, focusing intently on adjusting the strap of my own bag, avoiding his eyes entirely.

[story 2]

I was scrolling through old photos on my phone, a mindless distraction, when the news hit me. A picture of Liam standing in front of a postcard-worthy mountain range, with a caption underneath: 'New horizons await.' My breath hitched. My hands started trembling, and I instinctively brought the phone down, hiding the screen as if the image itself were contagious. I felt a sudden, intense heat rising up my neck, making my cheeks flush a deep crimson. When Liam finally cornered me by the vending machine, radiating cheerful energy, I just mumbled something about needing a drink, my gaze fixed firmly on the chipped paint on the machine. Every casual word he spoke about his new apartment felt like a spotlight, illuminating the sheer awkwardness of the situation.

[story 3]

The text message arrived at 10 PM. It was short, factual, and devastating: "Big news. I'm moving next month. To Oregon." I reread it three times, the words swimming before my eyes. I sat on the edge of my bed, hugging my knees tight to my chest, trying to physically shrink myself into the mattress. A strange tightness gripped my chest, making it hard to draw a full breath. When Liam called the next day, his voice was bright, full of plans for unpacking and exploring. I managed a strained, overly enthusiastic reply, nodding along too quickly. "That sounds amazing, man! Seriously, good luck with everything!" I forced a wide smile that felt brittle on my face, wishing the floor would simply open up and swallow me whole.
